event: signs of lower urinary tract disease
blood pressure readings too low to detect
initial resuscitation with lactated Ringer’s solution
point-of-care ultrasound showed free abdominal fluid
systolic blood pressure increased to 110 mmHg
ECG rhythm changed to sinus rhythm
temperature returned to normal within 2 h
peritoneal dialysis solution with dextrose and heparin
dialysate volume exchange increased from 10 ml/kg to 40 ml/kg
improvement in blood gas and electrolytes at 8 h
large volume of sediment in urinary bladder
pleural fluid analysis consistent with urothorax
culture results: Klebsiella pneumoniae, Mannheimia haemolytica, Micrococcus species, Staphylococcus species
